functions: Regulates homologous recombination repair (HRR)
Promotes DNA double-strand break end resection
Cell cycle checkpoint activation
Protein ubiquitination
Transcriptional regulation
Maintenance of genome integrity
Chromatin remodeling / epigenetic regulation
Supports DNA replication fidelity in early development
pubmed.druggability_notes: BRCA1-deficient (homologous recombination-deficient) tumors are sensitive to PARP inhibitors (olaparib, niraparib, fluzoparib) via synthetic lethality. Structural/functional domains include coiled-coil and RAD51-binding regions; secondary intragenic mutations restoring protein function are a key PARPi resistance mechanism. In BRCA1/2-WT ccRCC, SLFN11-driven proteasomal degradation of BRCA1 protein creates PARP-1 dependency that can be exploited with alkylating agents (temozolomide, streptozocin) plus PARP inhibitors.
cellular_states: Early embryonic / germ layer cells (maternal-to-zygotic transition)
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma cells

functions: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase mediating 'Lys-6'-linked polyubiquitin chain formation
Central role in DNA repair and cellular response to DNA damage
Coordinates DNA damage repair, ubiquitination and transcriptional regulation as part of BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer to maintain genomic stability
Regulates centrosomal microtubule nucleation
Required for cell cycle arrests in S-phase and G2 phase after ionizing irradiation
Required for FANCD2 targeting to sites of DNA damage
Inhibits lipid synthesis by binding inactive phosphorylated ACACA and preventing its dephosphorylation
Contributes to homologous recombination repair via interaction with PALB2 and loading of BRCA2-RAD51 machinery
pathways: Transcriptional regulation by RNA polymerase II
cellular_states: S-phase and G2/M cell cycle phases
uniprot.druggability_notes: Possesses a zinc-finger (RING) domain conferring metal-binding and E3 ubiquitin ligase (transferase) activity; functions as a transferase with catalytic ubiquitination activity. No explicit small-molecule binding pocket or drug class is described in the provided text.

opentargets.druggability_notes: No explicit druggability or binding-pocket information stated in the provided text. BRCA1 loss-of-function leads to HRR deficiency (pathways for defective HRR due to BRCA1 loss of function noted), which is the established basis for synthetic-lethality therapeutic strategies, though specific drug classes (e.g., PARP inhibitors) are not mentioned in this text.
